title: allium howellii
intro: Allium howellii, also known as Howell's onion, is a species of wild onion native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial plant with broad, flat leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Allium howellii include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion or decoction, and has been used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
